questions.explanation: RMSprop is not part of the exercise sheet, although Adam's second moment term plays a similar role.

questions.question: Which properties or methods must a fully trainable layer expose so it works with the finished framework? (Select all that apply)

options.C: A phase property that sets the phase of all other layers

questions.explanation: The phase property belongs to NeuralNetwork, which pushes the flag down into its layers; individual layers only carry testing_phase.

questions.question: Which statements about the exercise framework's NeuralNetwork class are true? (Select all that apply)

options.A: It computes the gradients itself rather than delegating to the layers
options.B: It has no explicit knowledge of the graph structure beyond a list of layers
options.C: It supports exactly one data source and one loss function
options.D: It stores the loss for every training iteration

questions.explanation: Each layer implements its own backward pass; the network only orchestrates the order in which they are called.

questions.question: Which statements about the exercise Conv layer are true? (Select all that apply)

options.A: 1x1 convolutions and 1-D convolutions must be handled correctly
options.B: The layer must use valid padding across the image plane
options.C: It must expose gradient_weights and gradient_bias
options.D: num_kernels determines the number of output channels

questions.explanation: The image plane uses same (zero) padding; it is the channel axis that is treated as a valid convolution.

questions.question: Which two libraries does exercise 0 ask you to import for calculation and visualisation?
questions.explanation: The conventional aliases np and plt are used throughout the exercise sheets.

questions.question: In which instance variable does every exercise 0 pattern class store its result?
questions.explanation: The unit tests read this member directly, so the name is fixed by the exercise.

questions.question: What does ImageGenerator.class_name(int_label) return?

options.A: The class name corresponding to the integer label
options.B: The one-hot encoding of the label
options.C: The number of samples in that class

questions.explanation: It is used to title the subplots produced by show().

questions.question: Which flags of the ImageGenerator constructor default to False?
questions.explanation: Augmentation and shuffling are opt-in, so the generator returns the raw data in file order unless asked otherwise.

questions.question: In which folder does the exercise framework place FullyConnected.py, ReLU.py and SoftMax.py?
questions.explanation: Optimizers.py, Loss.py and Constraints.py go into Optimization; the layers live in Layers.

questions.question: In which file and folder is the CrossEntropyLoss class implemented?
questions.explanation: The loss is grouped with the optimizers rather than the layers, mirroring the fact that it does not inherit BaseLayer.

questions.question: Which method adds a layer to the network and wires up its optimizer?
questions.explanation: It also initialises trainable layers with the network's stored weight and bias initializers.

questions.question: What does the 'loss' member of the NeuralNetwork class hold?

options.B: The current gradient of the loss
options.D: A list with the loss value of each training iteration

questions.explanation: The loss layer itself is kept separately in loss_layer; this list is what you plot to inspect convergence.

questions.question: What determines the number of output channels of the exercise Conv layer?
questions.explanation: Each kernel spans every input channel and contributes exactly one output feature map.

questions.question: Which form of pooling does exercise 2 ask you to implement?
questions.explanation: It is described as the most common form; average pooling only reappears in the PyTorch ResNet of exercise 4.

questions.question: Which distribution does the UniformRandom initializer draw from?
questions.explanation: It matches the simple initialisation used in exercise 1 before Xavier and He are introduced.

questions.question: Which file holds the Sgd, SgdWithMomentum and Adam classes?
questions.explanation: All three share the calculate_update interface, so they sit in one module.

questions.question: In which file are L1_Regularizer and L2_Regularizer implemented?
questions.explanation: The name reflects that they are norm constraints on the weights rather than layers.

questions.question: Which two trainable parameters does the BatchNormalization layer have?

options.D: the weights gamma and the bias beta

questions.explanation: The mean and variance are statistics estimated from the data, not parameters learned by the optimizer.

questions.question: Which layer of the exercise framework is described as most often used to regularize fully connected layers?
questions.explanation: Fully connected layers hold most of the parameters, which is where co-adaptation is most likely.

questions.question: Which two activation layers are added in exercise 3 for use inside recurrent cells?
questions.explanation: Both are bounded, which is what keeps the recurrent state from growing without limit.

questions.question: What is the name of the simple recurrent cell implemented in exercise 3?
questions.explanation: It is described as the simplest RNN cell and can be assembled modularly from layers you already built.

questions.question: How is the hidden state of the RNN layer initialised?
questions.explanation: It is reset to zeros for each new sequence unless memorize is True.

questions.question: Which library does exercise 4 use instead of the hand-written framework?
questions.explanation: It builds data flow graphs whose nodes are operations and whose edges are tensors.

questions.question: Which architecture do you implement in exercise 4?
questions.explanation: Its defining component is the ResBlock, a pair of convolution blocks augmented with a skip connection.

questions.question: What base class must the exercise 4 ResNet model inherit from?
questions.explanation: Subclassing Module is what registers the parameters and enables the standard training workflow.

questions.question: In the exercise 4 Trainer, what does a negative early_stopping_patience mean?

options.C: The patience is counted in batches rather than epochs
options.D: Early stopping is disabled, so an epoch count must be given instead

questions.explanation: The fit method asserts that either the patience or the epoch count is positive.
